summary: numpy.load in NumPy before allows arbitrary code execution via crafted .npy/.npz file due to unsafe pickle deserialization.
Multiple unpatched libraries bundled in older python base images carry known CVEs; base image should be pinned to a maintained, patched tag.
python-requests before 2.20.0 does not properly validate certificates when using a custom CA bundle, allowing MITM attacks.
Older Flask/Werkzeug versions are vulnerable to debugger PIN bypass and information disclosure if debug mode is enabled in production.
PyYAML yaml.load() allows arbitrary code execution via crafted YAML input due to unsafe default Loader. Use yaml.safe_load instead.
Jinja2 before 2.10.1 sandboxed environment can be bypassed via format string exploitation, potentially leading to code execution.
Django CommonMiddleware allows open redirect if APPEND_SLASH is enabled, via a crafted URL beginning with two slashes.
Django before 2.0.2 has a memory disclosure vulnerability via crafted image file uploads processed by the file upload handler.
Repeated failed SSH authentication attempts against privileged accounts (root/admin) from a single source IP is a classic credential-stuffing / brute-force pattern (aligns with MITRE ATT&CK T1110).
Automated scanning for common CMS admin paths, .env files, and path traversal payloads is a reconnaissance pattern preceding exploitation attempts (MITRE ATT&CK T1595).
